clusters.label: A
clusters.summary: Cluster A in Riverside / San Jacinto consists of 3 high-risk hexagons, with an average probability of 12.94% and a maximum probability of 14.15%. The area is experiencing extreme heat stress, with a 7-day average daytime surface temperature of 49.1°C and a drought index of 5.17. CAL FIRE is advising residents to be prepared for potential wildfires, with actions including: preparing defensible space, creating go-bags, and knowing evacuation routes.

clusters.label: B
clusters.summary: Cluster B in the San Bernardino Mountains consists of 8 high-risk hexagons, with an average probability of 11.7% and a maximum probability of 14.75%. The area is experiencing extreme heat stress, with a 7-day average daytime surface temperature of 44.7°C and a drought index of 5.21. CAL FIRE is advising residents to be prepared for potential wildfires, with actions including: preparing defensible space, creating go-bags, and knowing evacuation routes.
actions: Increase Type 1 engines in the area

overall_assessment: California is experiencing a heightened risk of wildfires, with 12 high-risk hexagons statewide. Residents in the Riverside / San Jacinto and San Bernardino Mountains areas are advised to take precautions and be prepared for potential wildfires.
